paragraph: The single-family market in Cresskill is moving at a measured pace. Thirty-five homes sold over the last six months at a median of $1,285,000, with a sale-to-list ratio of 0.99 and a median of 22 days on market. Over a third of homes sold above asking, which tells you the market isn't frenzied, but priced homes are finding buyers. The range is wide, from $460,000 to $4,299,000, so Cresskill has genuine diversity within the single-family category. Condos and townhouses are a much smaller slice of the market with only six sales at a median of $922,500, and they're moving faster at 15 days on market. Two-thirds of the condo sales went above asking, suggesting buyers are competing when the right unit hits the market. If you're shopping here, you're looking at a town where single-family homes dominate and sellers don't feel pressure to discount aggressively, but they're also not dictating terms. The best homes will still draw some competition, and if you find something that fits your needs and your budget, you shouldn't expect a long wait.
